content: For LGG patients, compute the average log10-transformed expression of the IGF2 gene across different histology types. Only include patients with valid IGF2 expression values and histology annotations that are not enclosed in square brackets. Report the final average values with at least four decimal places of precision.

message.content: For LGG patients, compute the average log10-transformed expression of the IGF2 gene across different histology types. Only include patients with valid IGF2 expression values and histology annotations that are not enclosed in square brackets. Report the final average values with at least four decimal places of precision.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command: duckdb '/workspace/DataAgentBench/query_PANCANCER_ATLAS/query_dataset/pancancer_molecular.db' "SELECT * FROM RNASeq_Expression WHERE Symbol='IGF2' LIMIT 5;"

lastPrompt: For LGG patients, compute the average log10-transformed expression of the IGF2 gene across different histology types. Only include patients with valid IGF2 expression values and histology annotations…

input.command:
psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U user -d pancancer_clinical -c "COPY (SELECT substring(\"Patient_description\" FROM 'TCGA-[A-Z0-9]{2}-[A-Z0-9]{4}') AS barcode, histological_type FROM clinical_info WHERE \"Patient_description\" ILIKE '%lower grade glioma%' AND histological_type NOT LIKE '[%]') TO STDOUT WITH CSV HEADER" > /tmp/lgg_patients.csv
wc -l /tmp/lgg_patients.csv

duckdb '/workspace/DataAgentBench/query_PANCANCER_ATLAS/query_dataset/pancancer_molecular.db' <<'EOF'
CREATE TEMP TABLE lgg AS SELECT * FROM read_csv_auto('/tmp/lgg_patients.csv');
SELECT l.histological_type,
       AVG(LOG10(r.normalized_count)) AS avg_log10_igf2,
       COUNT(*) AS n
FROM lgg l
JOIN RNASeq_Expression r ON l.barcode = r.ParticipantBarcode
WHERE r.Symbol = 'IGF2' AND r.normalized_count IS NOT NULL AND r.normalized_count > 0
GROUP BY l.histological_type;
EOF
input.description: Compute average log10 IGF2 expression by histology

input.command:
duckdb '/workspace/DataAgentBench/query_PANCANCER_ATLAS/query_dataset/pancancer_molecular.db' <<'EOF'
CREATE TEMP TABLE lgg AS SELECT * FROM read_csv_auto('/tmp/lgg_patients.csv');
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT l.barcode) AS lgg_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN r.ParticipantBarcode IS NOT NULL THEN l.barcode END) AS matched
FROM lgg l
LEFT JOIN RNASeq_Expression r ON l.barcode = r.ParticipantBarcode AND r.Symbol = 'IGF2';
EOF
input.description: Check how many LGG patients have IGF2 expression
Check IGF2 coverage and sample barcodes
Check gene coverage for a specific patient

│ TCGA-S9-A7J1       │ SNX3      │

input.command:
duckdb '/workspace/DataAgentBench/query_PANCANCER_ATLAS/query_dataset/pancancer_molecular.db' <<'EOF'
SELECT COUNT(*) total, COUNT(normalized_count) non_null, SUM(CASE WHEN normalized_count <= 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) zero_or_neg
FROM RNASeq_Expression WHERE Symbol='IGF2';
EOF

duckdb '/workspace/DataAgentBench/query_PANCANCER_ATLAS/query_dataset/pancancer_molecular.db' <<'EOF'
CREATE TEMP TABLE lgg AS SELECT * FROM read_csv_auto('/tmp/lgg_patients.csv');
SELECT l.histological_type,
       printf('%.6f', AVG(LOG10(r.normalized_count))) AS avg_log10_igf2,
       COUNT(*) AS n
FROM lgg l
JOIN RNASeq_Expression r ON l.barcode = r.ParticipantBarcode
WHERE r.Symbol = 'IGF2'
GROUP BY l.histological_type;
EOF

lastPrompt: For LGG patients, compute the average log10-transformed expression of the IGF2 gene across different histology types. Only include patients with valid IGF2 expression values and histology annotations…
